item.title: Shaping the Emerald City - NASA Science
item.description: The hills and basins of Seattle, Washington, have been sculpted by glaciers and modified by humans.

item.title: NASA Pushes New Wing Design to Find Structural Limits - NASA
item.description: NASA researchers recently put a new wing design, appearing long and thin with a lightweight structural design, through a series of grueling tests to find its

item.title: The Growing Crescent of Mars as NASA’s Psyche Mission Approaches - NASA Science
item.description: This composite of images taken by NASA’s Psyche mission shows the crescent of Mars grow as the spacecraft approached the planet for a gravity assist from May

item.description: In April 2026, NASA’s Office of the Chief Health and Medical Officer (OCHMO) initiated a working group to review updated VTE case information, additional data

item.title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Images Details of Martian Surface During Flyby - NASA Science
item.description: Captured by the multispectral imager instrument on NASA’s Psyche mission, this is an enhanced-color mosaic created from four individual images acquired on May

item.title: NASA Welcomes Mauritius as 70th Artemis Accords Signatory - NASA
item.description: The Republic of Mauritius has officially joined the global coalition committed to responsible space exploration, becoming the newest signatory and seventh

item.title: NASA Study Finds Near-Earth Asteroid Is Actually Comet - NASA
item.description: New research led by scientists at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California has revealed the identity of a puzzling near-Earth object by

item.title: New NASA Asteroid Observations Eliminate Chance of 2032 Lunar Impact - NASA Science
item.description: Using data from NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope observations collected on Feb. 18 and 26, experts from NASA’s Center for Near-Earth Object Studies at the

item.title: NASA Discovers Interstellar Comet Moving Through Solar System - NASA Science
item.description: On July 1, the NASA-funded ATLAS (Asteroid Terrestrial-impact Last Alert System) survey telescope in Rio Hurtado, Chile, first reported observations of a

item.title: Latest Calculations Conclude Asteroid 2024 YR4 Now Poses No Significant Threat to Earth in 2032 and Beyond - NASA Science
item.description: NASA has significantly lowered the risk of near-Earth asteroid 2024 YR4 as an impact threat to Earth for the foreseeable future. When first discovered, asteroid 2024 YR4 had a very small, but notable chance of impacting our planet in 2032. As observations of the asteroid continued to be submitted to the Minor Planet Center, experts …

item.title: All NASA News - NASA
item.description: Stay up-to-date with the latest content from NASA as we explore the universe and discover more about our home planet.

item.title: 2026 News Releases - NASA
item.description: Sign up to receive NASA news releases and other information.

item.title: Week Wraps with New Crew Adjustments and Human Research Studies - NASA
item.description: Crew swap activities are underway aboard the International Space Station as three new crewmates continue getting up to speed with living and working in microgravity and another Expedition 74 trio prepares to return to Earth. Meanwhile, scientific operations are back in full swing aboard the orbital outpost with lab residents studying how weightlessness affects the human body.

item.title: Expanded Crew Works Science, Microgravity Adaptation, and Next Crew Departure - NASA
item.description: The International Space Station is a busy place hosting ten crewmates from NASA, Roscosmos, and ESA (European Space Agency) through the end of July. The

item.title: News | Rocket Lab
item.description: Keep up with the latest from Rocket Lab.

item.title: Rocket Lab Delivers Mission Success for Space Force: Completes Historic Launch and On-Orbit Satellite Tracking Mission in Record Time | Rocket Lab
item.description:
Following a record-shattering responsive launch, Rocket Lab completes complex on-orbit rendezvous and proximity operations, proving the Company’s ability to track and inspect target satellites on short notice, delivering success for the U.S. Space Force’s VICTUS HAZE mission. 
LONG BEACH, Calif., 7…

item.title: Rocket Lab to Acquire Iridium in Historic Deal, Creating A Fully Vertically Integrated Space Powerhouse Primed for Growth | Rocket Lab
item.description: Rocket Lab to Acquire Iridium in Historic Deal, Creating A Fully Vertically Integrated Space Powerhouse Primed for Growth

item.title: Rocket Lab Completes 10th Consecutive Launch with 100% Mission Success for Synspective | Rocket Lab
item.description: Mahia, New Zealand. 27 June, 2026 – Rocket Lab Corporation (Nasdaq: RKLB), a global leader in launch services and space systems, today announced it has successfully placed another satellite into orbit for Synspective, achieving a significant milestone as the tenth dedicated launch for the…

item.title: NASA Selects Rocket Lab to Launch Sun, Earth Sciences Missions | Rocket Lab
item.description: The PolSIR and TSIS-2 missions will fly across three dedicated Electron launches from Q1 2027 to meet each mission’s time-sensitive requirements.

item.title: Rocket Lab Shatters Responsive Space Record: Launches U.S. Space Force VICTUS HAZE Mission in 16 Hours 42 Minutes | Rocket Lab
item.description:
Historic Speed: Rocket Lab launched the VICTUS HAZE mission just 16 hours and 42 minutes after receiving the U.S. Space Force’s Notice to Launch, beating the previous record by more than 10 hours and setting a new global standard in responsive space access.
End-to-End Space Capability: Marking a…

item.title: June 2026 Company Update
item.description: June brought continued momentum across the Terran R program, with major milestones across first stage qualification, second stage transport, and launch site readiness. Design In June, the team released 2,238 flight parts. Engineers remain focused on the first stage, releasing nearly all component

item.title: May 2026 Company Update
item.description: May brought a major milestone for the Terran R program, with the integrated second stage departing Long Beach and beginning its journey to NASA Stennis. Across the company, teams continued advancing work across design, build, test, and launch. Design This month, 1,455 flight parts were released,

item.title: April 2026 Company Update
item.description: April brought continued momentum across the Terran R program, with progress advancing across design, build, test, and launch.  Design This month, 2,055 flight parts were released, with a concerted effort on the stage one shipping assembly. Nearly all of the first stage structural compone

item.title: March 2026 Company Update
item.description: March brought continued progress across the Terran R program, with key milestones across design, manufacturing, testing, and launch preparations.  Design This month, the team released 2,207 flight parts, with a continued focus on completing the stage one shipping assembly. Work remains c

item.title: NASA's Chandra and IXPE Study Pulsar in Lighthouse Nebula - NASA
item.description: This composite image, released on July 9, 2026, shows the region around a pulsar – a neutron star with a strong magnetic field that spins incredibly fast –
